342	  \label{lem:gf_clear_one_denominator}
343	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.gf_clear_one_denominator}
344	  % NOTE (shared engine): the denominator-clearing performed here -- pushing finitely
345	  % many coefficients of one polynomial from K = Frac(A) down into a single
346	  % Localization.Away g -- is the same primitive the torsion reindex (L5b,
347	  % lem:gf_torsion_reindex) uses when it inverts the leading coefficient of the
348	  % Nagata-normalised annihilator. A prover who writes one general "clear finitely many
349	  % denominators into one Localization.Away g" helper can have both lemmas consume it.
350	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, normalisation step
351	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1755-L1759).
352	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
353	  % "If $g\ne 0$ in $A$ is chosen to be a `common denominator' for
354	  %  coefficients of equations of integral dependence
355	  %  satisfied by a finite set of
356	  %  algebra generators for $K\otimes_AB$ over $K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$,
357	  %  we see that $B_g$ is finite over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$."
358	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold creates the decl pinned above with this signature).
359	  %   Let K := FractionRing A and A_g := Localization.Away g; the canonical map
360	  %   A_g -> K (induced because powers of g are non-zero-divisors of A) extends along
361	  %   MvPolynomial.map to MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g -> MvPolynomial (Fin n) K.
362	  %     (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (n : ℕ)
363	  %       (p : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (FractionRing A)) :
364	  %       ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0),
365	  %         p ∈ Set.range
366	  %           (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap (Localization.Away g) (FractionRing A)))
367	  % NOTE (encoding): there is NO canonical `Algebra (Localization.Away g)
368	  %   (FractionRing A)` instance, so the landed Lean does not use the literal
369	  %   `algebraMap (Localization.Away g) (FractionRing A)` above. It encodes the same map
370	  %   as `IsLocalization.map (FractionRing A) (RingHom.id A) hle` with
371	  %   `hle : powers g ≤ comap id (nonZeroDivisors A)` — mathematically identical, just the
372	  %   well-typed form. The proof is axiom-clean.
373	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (common denominator).}
374	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain with fraction field \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\), and let
375	  \(p \in K[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) be a polynomial with coefficients in \(K\) (for instance,
376	  one coefficient polynomial of an equation of integral dependence over
377	  \(K[b_1, \dots, b_n]\)). Then there exists \(g \in A\), \(g \ne 0\), such that \(p\)
378	  lies in the image of the coefficient-extension map
379	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \to K[X_1, \dots, X_n]\); equivalently, \(p\) has all of its
380	  coefficients in \(A_g = A[g^{-1}] \subseteq K\).
381	\end{lemma}
382	
383	\begin{proof}
384	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1755-L1759).
385	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (common denominator).}
386	  The polynomial \(p\) has finitely many non-zero coefficients
387	  \(c_1, \dots, c_r \in K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\). Each \(c_j\) is a fraction
388	  \(c_j = a_j / s_j\) with \(a_j \in A\) and \(s_j \in A \setminus \{0\}\). Put
389	  \(g := s_1 \cdots s_r \in A\); since \(A\) is a domain and each \(s_j \ne 0\), also
390	  \(g \ne 0\). For each \(j\) the denominator \(s_j\) divides \(g\), so
391	  \(c_j = a_j / s_j\) lies in the subring \(A_g = A[g^{-1}] \subseteq K\). Hence every
392	  coefficient of \(p\) lies in \(A_g\), and \(p\) is the coefficient-extension of a
393	  polynomial in \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\).
394	\end{proof}

453	  \(A\)-algebra that is a domain with \(B_K = K \otimes_A B \ne 0\). Write

455	  and its image. Then there exist a natural number \(n\), an element \(g \in A\) with
456	  \(g \ne 0\), and an injective \(A_g\)-algebra homomorphism
457	  \[
458	    \varphi : A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \longrightarrow B_g,
459	  \]
460	  where \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) is the polynomial ring on \(n\) algebraically
461	  independent variables, such that \(B_g\) is module-finite over the image polynomial
462	  subalgebra via \(\varphi\). The injectivity of \(\varphi\) records the algebraic
463	  independence of the generators \(b_j := \varphi(X_j)\), and module-finiteness is
464	  taken over the algebra structure that \(\varphi\) puts on \(B_g\).

467	\begin{proof}
468	  \uses{lem:noether_normalization_fg, lem:gf_clear_one_denominator}
469	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1747-L1758).
470	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (Noether normalisation with denominators).}
471	  % NOTE: the denominator-clearing middle of this proof is a Finset-fold over the finite
472	  % generating set sigma, but each fold step is the Mathlib collapsing lemma
473	  % IsIntegral.exists_multiple_integral_of_isLocalization applied to the generator AS A
474	  % WHOLE (not to its individual coefficient polynomials via gf_clear_one_denominator).
475	  % For each generator x, that lemma yields a single a_x in A^0 (nonzero, since A is a
476	  % domain) with C(a_x) * (1 (x) x) integral over MvPolynomial A; set g1 := prod_x a_x and
477	  % g := g0 * g1. The AlgHom assembly below then runs over A_g exactly as before.
478	
479	  \emph{Step 1 — Noether normalisation over \(K\).} Apply Noether normalisation over the
480	  field \(K\) (\cref{lem:noether_normalization_fg}) to the finite-type \(K\)-algebra
481	  \(B_K = K \otimes_A B\): there are algebraically independent
482	  \(\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n \in B_K\) with \(B_K\) module-finite over
483	  \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\). Clearing denominators, each \(\bar b_j\) is
484	  represented by some \(b_j \in B\) (writing \(1 \otimes b\) as \(b\)).
485	
486	  \emph{Step 2 — clear the integral-dependence denominators to a single \(g\).} Fix a
487	  finite set \(\sigma\) of algebra generators of \(B_K\) over
488	  \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\). By Step 1 each generator's image
489	  \(1 \otimes x \in B_K\) is integral over \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\). For each
490	  \(x \in \sigma\) apply the Mathlib collapsing lemma
491	  \texttt{IsIntegral.exists\_multiple\_integral\_of\_isLocalization} (with
492	  \(R = \mathrm{MvPoly}\,A\), \(R_m = \mathrm{MvPoly}\,K\), \(S = B_K\), and the localizing
493	  submonoid the image of \(A^{0}\)): this returns a single
494	  \(a_x \in A^{0}\) (non-zero, as \(A\) is a domain) such that \(C(a_x)\cdot(1\otimes x)\)
495	  is integral over \(\mathrm{MvPoly}\,A = A[b_1, \dots, b_n]\). Multiplying over the finite
496	  set, set \(g_1 := \prod_{x \in \sigma} a_x\) and \(g := g_0 \cdot g_1\) (with \(g_0\) the
497	  Step-1 denominator witness). After inverting \(g\), every chosen generator becomes
498	  integral over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\) and the generators generate \(B_g\) as an algebra;
499	  hence \(B_g\) is module-finite over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\). (The single-coefficient
500	  lemma \cref{lem:gf_clear_one_denominator} provides the same denominator-clearing primitive
501	  at the level of one coefficient polynomial; the landed proof discharges a whole generator
502	  at once via the collapsing lemma, which folds that primitive over the coefficients
503	  internally.)
504	
505	  \emph{Step 3 — AlgHom assembly.} The algebra homomorphism \(\varphi\) of the statement
506	  is the \(A_g\)-algebra map \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \to B_g\) sending \(X_j \mapsto b_j\),
507	  where \(b_j \in B_g\) is the image of \(b_j \in B\). We establish injectivity and
508	  module-finiteness through a comparison with the situation over \(K\).
509	
510	  \emph{Step 3a — the comparison map \(\nu : B_g \to B_K\).} Since \(g \ne 0\) in the
511	  domain \(A\) and \(A \subseteq K\), the image of \(g\) is a unit of the field \(K\), so
512	  it is invertible in \(B_K = K \otimes_A B\). Hence the canonical \(A\)-algebra map
513	  \(B \to B_K\), \(b \mapsto 1 \otimes b\), sends \(g\) to a unit and, by the universal
514	  property of localisation, factors through a canonical \(A\)-algebra map
515	  \[
516	    \nu : B_g = B[g^{-1}] \longrightarrow B_K, \qquad \nu(b/g^k) = (1 \otimes b)\,(1 \otimes g)^{-k}.
517	  \]
518	  The map \(\nu\) is injective. Indeed, \(B\) is a domain, and both \(B_g\) and \(B_K\) are
519	  localisations of \(B\) at multiplicative subsets of the non-zerodivisors: \(B_g\) inverts
520	  the powers of \(g\), while \(B_K = K \otimes_A B\) inverts (in particular) the images of
521	  all non-zero elements of \(A\), a multiplicative set containing the powers of \(g\). A
522	  localisation of a domain at a set of non-zerodivisors is injective into any further such
523	  localisation. Concretely, the natural map \(B \to B_K\) is injective on the domain \(B\)
524	  (its kernel is the set of \(b\) annihilated by some non-zero element of \(A\), which in a
525	  domain over which \(A\) acts faithfully is \(0\)), so \(\nu(b/g^k) = 0\) forces
526	  \(1 \otimes b = 0\), hence \(b = 0\) and \(b/g^k = 0\). Under \(\nu\) the lifts
527	  \(b_j \in B_g\) of the Noether generators map to the elements \(\bar b_j \in B_K\) of
528	  Step 1: \(\nu(b_j) = \bar b_j\).
529	
530	  \emph{Step 3b — injectivity of \(\varphi\) by algebraic-independence descent
531	  \(K \to A_g\).} Injectivity of the \(A_g\)-algebra map
532	  \(\varphi : A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \to B_g\), \(X_j \mapsto b_j\), is equivalent to the
533	  algebraic independence of the family \((b_j)\) over \(A_g\): a non-zero element of the
534	  kernel is exactly a non-trivial \(A_g\)-polynomial relation \(P(b_1, \dots, b_n) = 0\).
535	  The localisation \(A_g = A[g^{-1}] \subseteq K\) embeds in the fraction field, giving an
536	  injection of \(A_g\)-algebras \(A_g \hookrightarrow K\). Now suppose
537	  \(P \in A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) is non-zero with \(P(b_1, \dots, b_n) = 0\) in \(B_g\).
538	  Applying the injective map \(\nu\) and using \(\nu(b_j) = \bar b_j\) together with the fact
539	  that \(\nu\) is an \(A\)-algebra map extending scalars to \(K\), we obtain
540	  \(P^K(\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n) = 0\) in \(B_K\), where \(P^K\) is the image of \(P\)
541	  under the coefficient inclusion \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \hookrightarrow K[X_1, \dots, X_n]\).
542	  Since \(A_g \hookrightarrow K\) is injective, \(P \ne 0\) gives \(P^K \ne 0\); but the
543	  \(\bar b_j\) are algebraically independent over \(K\) (Step 1), so the only \(K\)-polynomial
544	  vanishing on them is \(0\), a contradiction. Hence no non-trivial relation exists, \((b_j)\)
545	  is algebraically independent over \(A_g\), and \(\varphi\) is injective.
546	
547	  \emph{Step 3c — module-finiteness of \(B_g\) over \(\operatorname{im}\varphi\).} By Step 1,
548	  \(B_K\) is module-finite over \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\); fix a finite set of algebra
549	  generators of \(B_K\) over \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\) (Step 2). Each such generator
550	  satisfies a monic equation of integral dependence whose coefficients are polynomials in
551	  \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\). The element \(g\) of Step 2 was chosen as a common
552	  denominator for the (finitely many) coefficient polynomials of these equations, so that
553	  after inverting \(g\) every such coefficient already lies in
554	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n] = \operatorname{im}\varphi\). Each chosen generator is the
555	  \(\nu\)-image of an element of \(B_g\), and its integral-dependence equation is the
556	  \(\nu\)-image of the corresponding monic equation holding in \(B_g\) with coefficients in
557	  \(\operatorname{im}\varphi\); since \(\nu\) is injective, that equation holds in \(B_g\)
558	  itself. Thus each generator of \(B_g\) is integral over the subalgebra
559	  \(\operatorname{im}\varphi\), and these generators generate \(B_g\) as an
560	  \(\operatorname{im}\varphi\)-algebra. A finite-type algebra that is integral over a
561	  subalgebra is module-finite over it; hence \(B_g\) is module-finite over
562	  \(\operatorname{im}\varphi = A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\). Transported along the injection
563	  \(\varphi\), this is exactly module-finiteness of \(B_g\) over \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\) for
564	  the algebra structure that \(\varphi\) puts on \(B_g\), as claimed. This recovers the
565	  source's conclusion that \(B_g\) is finite over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\).
566	\end{proof}

568	\begin{lemma}\leanok
569	[injectivity of a localisation lift]
570	  \label{lem:gf_isLocalization_lift_injective}
571	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.isLocalization_lift_injective}
572	  Let \(S\) be a localisation of a ring \(R\) at a multiplicative set \(M\), and let
573	  \(g : R \to P\) be a ring homomorphism sending every element of \(M\) to a unit of
574	  \(P\), so that it induces the lift \(\widehat g : S \to P\). If both the localisation
575	  map \(R \to S\) and \(g\) itself are injective, then \(\widehat g\) is injective.
576	\end{lemma}
577	\begin{proof}
578	  The lift is characterised by \(\widehat g(r/m) = g(r)\,g(m)^{-1}\). By the universal
579	  criterion for injectivity of a localisation lift, \(\widehat g\) is injective iff for
580	  all \(r_1, r_2 \in R\) the equality of localisation images
581	  \((r_1 \mapsto S) = (r_2 \mapsto S)\) implies \(g(r_1) = g(r_2)\). Both the hypothesis
582	  and the conclusion of that implication reduce, via injectivity of \(R \to S\) and of
583	  \(g\), to the single condition \(r_1 = r_2\); hence the implication holds and
584	  \(\widehat g\) is injective. (This helper is applied twice in
585	  \cref{lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators}, to the comparison maps \(\nu : B_g \to B_K\)
586	  and \(\psi : A_g \to K\).)
587	\end{proof}

589	\begin{lemma}
590	
591	
592	\leanok
593	[L5a — the generic-rank short exact sequence]
594	  \label{lem:gf_generic_rank_ses}
595	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.gf_generic_rank_ses}
596	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, inductive step
597	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1760-L1765).
598	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
599	  % "Let $m$ be the generic rank of the finite module $B_g$ over the
600	  %  domain $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Then we have a short exact
601	  %  sequence of $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$-modules of the form
602	  %  $$0\to A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0$$
603	  %  where $T$ is a finite torsion module over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$."
604	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold creates the decl pinned above with this signature).
605	  %   Write P_d := MvPolynomial (Fin d) A.
606	  %     (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
607	  %       (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
608	  %       [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
609	  %       [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
610	  %       [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
611	  %       ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
612	  %                        →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
613	  %         Function.Injective φ ∧
614	  %         Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ)
615	  %   witness: m = Module.finrank (FractionRing P_d)
616	  %                  (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors P_d) N), the generic rank;
617	  %            φ = Fintype.linearCombination P_d v, with v : Fin m → N the
618	  %            denominator-cleared lifts of a FractionRing P_d-basis of the localisation.
619	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (generic-rank SES).}
620	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(d \ge 0\), and \(N\) a finite module over the
621	  polynomial ring \(P_d := A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) (with its induced \(A\)-module structure
622	  through the scalar tower \(A \to P_d \to N\)). Then there exist \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) and
623	  an injective \(P_d\)-linear map \(\varphi : P_d^{\oplus m} \to N\) whose cokernel
624	  \(T := N / \operatorname{im}\varphi\) is a torsion \(P_d\)-module. Equivalently, there is
625	  a short exact sequence of \(P_d\)-modules
626	  \[
627	    0 \to P_d^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\ \varphi\ } N \to T \to 0,
628	    \qquad T \text{ torsion}.
629	  \]
630	
631	  The integer \(m\) is the \emph{generic rank} of \(N\): the dimension of the
632	  \(\mathrm{Frac}(P_d)\)-vector space
633	  \(\mathrm{Frac}(P_d) \otimes_{P_d} N\), i.e.\ of the localisation of \(N\) at the
634	  non-zero-divisors of the domain \(P_d\). This step lives over \(P_d\) directly —
635	  \emph{no inversion of any element \(g \in A\) is required here}; the element \(g\) of
636	  Nitsure's argument enters only at the reindexing step \cref{lem:gf_torsion_reindex}.
637	\end{lemma}
638	
639	\begin{proof}
640	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1760-L1765).
641	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (generic-rank SES).}
642	  Write \(P_d = A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\); since \(A\) is a noetherian domain, so is \(P_d\)
643	  (Hilbert basis theorem, and a polynomial ring over a domain is a domain). Let
644	  \(Q = \mathrm{Frac}(P_d)\) be its fraction field and
645	  \(N_Q := Q \otimes_{P_d} N\) the localisation of \(N\) at the non-zero-divisors of
646	  \(P_d\); as \(N\) is finite over \(P_d\), \(N_Q\) is a finite-dimensional
647	  \(Q\)-vector space. Put \(m := \dim_Q N_Q\), the generic rank.
648	
649	  Choose a \(Q\)-basis of \(N_Q\) and clear denominators: each basis vector is a
650	  \(Q\)-unit multiple of the image of some \(v_i \in N\), so the images of
651	  \(v_1, \dots, v_m \in N\) again form a \(Q\)-basis of \(N_Q\). In particular the family
652	  \((v_i)_{i}\) is linearly independent over \(Q\); restricting scalars along the
653	  injection \(P_d \hookrightarrow Q\), it is linearly independent over \(P_d\). Let
654	  \(\varphi : P_d^{\oplus m} \to N\) be the \(P_d\)-linear map
655	  \((c_1, \dots, c_m) \mapsto \sum_i c_i v_i\). Linear independence of \((v_i)\) over
656	  \(P_d\) makes \(\varphi\) injective.
657	
658	  Set \(T := N / \operatorname{im}\varphi\). Because the images of \(v_1, \dots, v_m\)
659	  already span \(N_Q\) over \(Q\), the localised map \(\varphi \otimes Q : Q^{\oplus m}
660	  \to N_Q\) is an isomorphism, so \(Q \otimes_{P_d} T = 0\). A finite \(P_d\)-module whose
661	  localisation at the non-zero-divisors of \(P_d\) vanishes is torsion; hence \(T\) is a
662	  torsion \(P_d\)-module. This is the asserted short exact sequence.
663	\end{proof}

2178	    Module.Finite (Localization S) (LocalizedModule S M) := by
2179	  classical
2180	  let e : N ≃ₗ[R] LocalizedModule S M :=

2198	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]

2201	        ← Algebra.smul_def, ← map_smul ψ, hsa, ψ.commutes, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]

2210	    rwa [e.apply_symm_apply] at this
2211	  intro z hz
2212	  induction hz using Submodule.span_induction with
2213	  | mem z hz => exact Submodule.subset_span ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
2214	  | zero => simp

2253	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive

32	- **Mathlib idiom**: use the adjunction `tilde.functor R ⊣ moduleSpecΓFunctor` and the fact
33	  that a **faithful** functor reflects epimorphisms. Cite:

37	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction : tilde.functor R ⊣ moduleSpecΓFunctor` (Tilde.lean:279).
38	  - counit `= AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓNatTrans : moduleSpecΓFunctor ⋙ tilde.functor ⟶ 𝟭`
39	    (Tilde.lean:248), whose **`.app M := M.fromTildeΓ` definitionally** (Tilde.lean:250) — so

44	  - `ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective` (Epi in `ModuleCat R` ↔ surjective).
45	- **Project's current path**: hand-rolled object-wise isos (`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`,
46	  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_*`) plus a blueprint hand-wave that affine Γ is exact.
47	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost — the object-wise machinery cannot carry an epi; the global
48	  adjunction can, and is already in Mathlib.
49	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB.
50	
51	### Decision: where do `[IsIso G.fromTildeΓ]`/`[IsIso F.fromTildeΓ]` come from
52	- **Mathlib idiom**: `AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff : IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ (tilde.functor R).essImage M`
53	  (Tilde.lean) and `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (a presented module forces it). For the
54	  free source `O_V^{⊕I}` (presented) this is immediate; for the finite-type `F` the project's
55	  G1-core (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict`, `QuotScheme.lean:614`) supplies it
56	  from quasi-coherence.
57	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (project's quasicoherence→IsIso bridge is the right feeder; Mathlib has
58	  no `IsQuasicoherent`-predicate version, only essImage / IsLocalizing).
59	
60	## Proof skeleton (Mathlib-grounded, ~12 lines)
61	```lean
62	open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry in
63	theorem gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi {R : CommRingCat} {G F : (Spec R).Modules}
64	    (π : G ⟶ F) [Epi π] [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
65	    Function.Surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom := by
66	  rw [← ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective]
67	  -- counit naturality: (Γ⋙~).map π ≫ F.fromTildeΓ = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π
68	  have hnat := (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓNatTrans (R := R)).naturality π
69	  -- ⇒ ~(Γ π) = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ  : composite of iso, epi, iso ⇒ Epi
70	  haveI : Epi ((tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π)) := by
71	    have h : (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π)
72	           = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ := by
73	      -- rearrange hnat (fromTildeΓNatTrans.app X = X.fromTildeΓ, (𝟭).map π = π)
74	      simp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.id_map] at hnat
75	      rw [← hnat]; simp
76	    rw [h]; infer_instance
77	  -- tilde.functor is faithful ⇒ reflects epis
78	  exact (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ‹_›
79	```
80	(Exact `simp`/`rw` glue may need a line or two of tuning, but every ingredient is a named
81	Mathlib decl. `Γ(π)` in the blueprint = `moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π`; its `.hom` is the B-linear map.)
82	
83	## Recommendation
84	Restate `gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi` against `moduleSpecΓFunctor` + `tilde.adjunction`, taking
85	`[Epi π]` and the two `IsIso _.fromTildeΓ` instances as hypotheses. Drop the "affine Γ is exact"
86	hand-wave in the blueprint proof and replace it with: *the tilde counit is iso on quasi-coherent
87	objects, so the counit-naturality square makes `~(Γπ)` an epi; `tilde.functor` is faithful, hence
88	reflects epimorphisms, so `Γπ` is epi in `ModuleCat R`, i.e. surjective.* This is the structural
89	content of "H¹(affine,qcoh)=0" delivered for free by Mathlib's adjunction — no cohomology build.
90	Mechanism ranking: **(b)+(a) merged via the adjunction counit is the answer; (c) genuine
91	H¹-vanishing is NOT needed.**

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry

open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry in
theorem gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi {R : CommRingCat} {G F : (Spec R).Modules}
    (π : G ⟶ F) [Epi π] [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
    Function.Surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom := by
  rw [← ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective]
  refine (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ?_
  have hnat := (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓNatTrans (R := R)).naturality π
  simp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.id_map] at hnat
  change (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π) ≫ F.fromTildeΓ
       = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π at hnat
  have h : (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π)
         = (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ := by
    rw [IsIso.eq_comp_inv]; exact hnat
  rw [h]
  haveI : Epi (inv F.fromTildeΓ) := inferInstance
  exact epi_comp (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) (inv F.fromTildeΓ)

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry

open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry in
theorem gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi {R : CommRingCat} {G F : (Spec R).Modules}
    (π : G ⟶ F) [Epi π] [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
    Function.Surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom := by
  rw [← ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective]
  refine (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ?_
  have hnat := (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓNatTrans (R := R)).naturality π
  simp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.id_map] at hnat
  change (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π) ≫ F.fromTildeΓ
       = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π at hnat
  have h : (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π)
         = (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ :=
    (IsIso.eq_comp_inv F.fromTildeΓ).mpr hnat
  rw [h]
  haveI : Epi (inv F.fromTildeΓ) := inferInstance
  exact epi_comp (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) (inv F.fromTildeΓ)

1567	
1568	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1569	[Half 1 — G1 locality reduction over a finite basic-open cover]
1570	  \label{lem:gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover}
1571	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover}
1572	  \uses{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen, lem:finite_localizedModule_transfer,
1573	        lem:module_finite_of_localizationSpan, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
1574	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof, gluing step
1575	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2107-L2109).
1576	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1577	  % "Thus $M_{f_i}$ is finitely generated for each $i$. Hence we conclude by
1578	  %  Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover}."
1579	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (gluing step,
1580	  Algebra Lemma \texttt{lemma-cover}).}
1581	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(X\), and
1582	  \(W \subseteq X\) an affine open. Suppose \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) is a finite
1583	  family with \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) (so the basic opens \(D(g)\),
1584	  \(g \in t\), cover \(W\)), and that for each \(g \in t\) the section module
1585	  \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) is finite over \(\Gamma(X, D(g))\). Then \(\Gamma(\F, W)\)
1586	  is a finite \(\Gamma(X, W)\)-module.
1587	\end{lemma}
1588	
1589	\begin{proof}
1590	  \uses{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen, lem:finite_localizedModule_transfer,
1591	        lem:module_finite_of_localizationSpan, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
1592	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (gluing step).}
1593	  By the local-on-a-spanning-family criterion \cref{lem:module_finite_of_localizationSpan}
1594	  it suffices to show, for each \(g \in t\), that the localization of
1595	  \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) at \(g\) is finite over the localization
1596	  \((\Gamma(X, W))_g\). Fix \(g \in t\). The basic open \(D(g)\) is affine, and the
1597	  restriction \(\Gamma(X, W) \to \Gamma(X, D(g))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(X, D(g))\) as
1598	  the away-localization \((\Gamma(X, W))_g\)
1599	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}); moreover the section restriction
1600	  \(\Gamma(\F, W) \to \Gamma(\F, D(g))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) as the
1601	  localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,g)^{-1}\,\Gamma(\F, W)\)
1602	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}). Thus \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) is a
1603	  model of the localized module, finite over \(\Gamma(X, D(g)) = (\Gamma(X, W))_g\)
1604	  by hypothesis; the model-transfer lemma
1605	  \cref{lem:finite_localizedModule_transfer} carries this finiteness to the
1606	  canonical localized module of \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) at the powers of \(g\), over
1607	  \((\Gamma(X, W))_g\). This supplies the per-element hypothesis, and
1608	  \cref{lem:module_finite_of_localizationSpan} concludes that \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is
1609	  finite over \(\Gamma(X, W)\).
1610	\end{proof}
1611	
1612	\begin{lemma}[Half 2, seam 1 — finite type yields a finite cover of globally-generated affines]
1613	  \label{lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}
1614	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}
1615	  % LEAN STATUS: base-case seam, project-built (Mathlib-absent). Proposed decl
1616	  % AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated; extracts a
1617	  % standard finite cover of W on whose members F is globally generated, from the
1618	  % SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType local-generators datum refined by quasi-compactness
1619	  % of the affine W.
1620	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof
1621	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2101-L2107).
1622	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1623	  % "Assume $\widetilde M$ is a finite type $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. This means
1624	  %  there exists an open covering of $X$ such that $\widetilde M$ restricted to
1625	  %  the members of this covering is globally generated by finitely many sections.
1626	  %  Thus there also exists a standard open covering
1627	  %  $X = \bigcup_{i = 1, \ldots, n} D(f_i)$ such that $\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$
1628	  %  is generated by finitely many sections."
1629	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (standard
1630	  cover of generated affines).}
1631	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules of finite type,
1632	  and \(W \subseteq X\) an affine open. Then there is a finite family
1633	  \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) such that for
1634	  each \(g \in t\) the basic open \(D(g)\) is affine and \(\F|_{D(g)}\) is globally
1635	  generated by finitely many sections; equivalently, for each \(g \in t\) there is
1636	  a finite index set \(I_g\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_{D(g)}\)-modules
1637	  \(\OO_{D(g)}^{\oplus I_g} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_{D(g)}\).
1638	\end{lemma}
1639	
1640	\begin{proof}
1641	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (standard cover of generated affines).}
1642	  Finite type of \(\F\) means that \(X\) has an open covering on each member of
1643	  which \(\F\) is generated by finitely many sections. Intersecting this covering
1644	  with the affine \(W\) and refining the resulting cover of \(W\) by standard basic
1645	  opens, every point of \(W\) lies in a basic open \(D(g) \subseteq W\) on which
1646	  \(\F\) is generated by finitely many sections. Since \(W\) is affine, hence
1647	  quasi-compact, finitely many such basic opens \(D(g_1), \dots, D(g_n)\) already
1648	  cover \(W\); collect their defining elements into the finite family
1649	  \(t = \{g_1, \dots, g_n\} \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\). That the \(D(g_i)\) cover
1650	  \(W = \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) is exactly the condition
1651	  \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\). A basic open of an affine is affine, and being
1652	  generated by a finite family of sections is precisely the existence of an
1653	  epimorphism from the corresponding finite free module. This is Stacks' passage
1654	  from the local-generation datum to a standard finite cover of generated affines.
1655	\end{proof}
1656	
1657	\begin{lemma}[Affine $\widetilde{(-)}\dashv\Gamma$ adjunction with its $\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}$ counit]
1658	  \label{lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib}
1659	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction}
1660	  \mathlibok
1661	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}).}
1662	  On the affine scheme \(\Spec R\) the associated-sheaf functor
1663	  \(\widetilde{(-)} : \mathrm{Mod}\,R \to (\Spec R).\mathrm{Modules}\) is left adjoint to the
1664	  affine section functor \(\Gamma : (\Spec R).\mathrm{Modules} \to \mathrm{Mod}\,R\) (Mathlib's
1665	  \(\mathrm{moduleSpecΓFunctor}\), evaluation of a sheaf of modules at the top open). The counit of
1666	  this adjunction is the natural transformation
1667	  \(\Gamma(-)\,\widetilde{\phantom{x}} \to \mathrm{id}\) whose component at a sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\)
1668	  is \(\mathcal{M}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{M}, \top)} \to \mathcal{M}\), and
1669	  the left adjoint \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is faithful.
1670	\end{lemma}
1671	
1672	\begin{lemma}[Half 2, seam 2 — $\Gamma$ on affine quasi-coherent modules sends epis to surjections]
1673	  \label{lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}
1674	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}
1675	  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization, lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib,
1676	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
1677	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof
1678	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2106-L2107) — the implication
1679	  % "$\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$ generated by finitely many sections $\Rightarrow$
1680	  % $M_{f_i}$ finitely generated" is exactly section-level surjectivity of a sheaf
1681	  % epi over an affine.
1682	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1683	  % "such that $\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$ is generated by finitely many sections.
1684	  %  Thus $M_{f_i}$ is finitely generated for each $i$."
1685	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (sections of a
1686	  generated sheaf over an affine).}
1687	  Let \(V\) be an affine open of a scheme, with \(B = \Gamma(X, V)\) and
1688	  \(V \cong \Spec B\), and let \(\pi : \mathcal{G} \to \F\) be an epimorphism of
1689	  quasi-coherent sheaves of modules over \(V\). Then the induced map on sections
1690	  \(\Gamma(\pi) : \Gamma(\mathcal{G}, V) \to \Gamma(\F, V)\) is a surjective
1691	  \(B\)-module map.
1692	\end{lemma}
1693	
1694	\begin{proof}
1695	  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization, lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib,
1696	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
1697	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (sections of a generated sheaf over an
1698	  affine).}
1699	  Write \(V \cong \Spec B\) and let \(\Gamma\) be the affine section functor on
1700	  \((\Spec B).\mathrm{Modules}\), so that \(\Gamma(\pi) = \Gamma(\mathcal{G}, V) \to
1701	  \Gamma(\F, V)\) is the map in question. By \cref{lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib} the
1702	  associated-sheaf functor \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is left adjoint to \(\Gamma\), with counit the
1703	  natural transformation whose component at a sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\) is
1704	  \(\mathcal{M}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{M}, V)} \to \mathcal{M}\).
1705	
1706	  Because \(\mathcal{G}\) and \(\F\) are quasi-coherent, the qcoh-sections descent
1707	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}, fed through the counit-iso assembly
1708	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}) makes both counit components
1709	  \(\mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) and \(\F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) isomorphisms. Naturality of the
1710	  counit on \(\pi\) gives the commuting square
1711	  \[
1712	    \widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)} \circ \mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1}
1713	      = \F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1} \circ \pi,
1714	    \qquad\text{equivalently}\qquad
1715	    \widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)}
1716	      = \F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1} \circ \pi \circ \mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}.
1717	  \]
1718	  The right-hand side is a composite of an isomorphism, the epimorphism \(\pi\), and an
1719	  isomorphism, hence is itself an epimorphism. Thus \(\widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)}\) is an epimorphism
1720	  of sheaves of modules. Since the left adjoint \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is faithful, it reflects
1721	  epimorphisms; therefore \(\Gamma(\pi)\) is an epimorphism in \(\mathrm{Mod}\,B\), i.e. the
1722	  \(B\)-linear map \(\Gamma(\pi)\) is surjective.
1723	
1724	  This is the structural content of the vanishing of higher quasi-coherent cohomology on an
1725	  affine — the failure of \(\Gamma\) to be right exact would obstruct exactly this surjectivity —
1726	  obtained here directly from the affine adjunction without invoking a cohomology computation.
1727	\end{proof}

1743	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB.}
1744	  Let \(V\) be an affine open of a scheme \(X\), write \(B = \Gamma(X, V)\), and let
1745	  \(\F\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules. Suppose \(\F\) is globally generated
1746	  by finitely many sections on \(V\): there is a finite index set \(I\) and an
1747	  epimorphism of \(\OO_V\)-modules \(\OO_V^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_V\).
1748	  Then \(\Gamma(\F, V)\) is a finite \(B\)-module.
1749	\end{lemma}

1755	  \(\OO_V^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_V\) over the affine \(V\): the section
1756	  functor yields a surjective \(B\)-module map
1757	  \(\Gamma(\OO_V^{\oplus I}, V) \to \Gamma(\F, V)\). The source is the sections of a
1758	  finite free \(\OO_V\)-module, so \(\Gamma(\OO_V^{\oplus I}, V) \cong B^{\oplus I}\)
1759	  is a finite free \(B\)-module. A finite module surjecting onto \(\Gamma(\F, V)\)
1760	  forces \(\Gamma(\F, V)\) to be finite, by
1761	  \cref{lem:module_finite_of_surjective_mathlib}. Hence \(\Gamma(\F, V)\) is a finite
1762	  \(B\)-module.
1763	\end{proof}

1785	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB.}
1786	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\F : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of
1787	  modules with \(\F\) of finite type. Then for every affine open \(W \subseteq X\), the
1788	  section module \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is a finite module over \(\Gamma(X, W)\).

1793	        lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated,

1798	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB.}
1799	  Fix an affine open \(W \subseteq X\). Cover extraction
1800	  \cref{lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated} gives a finite
1801	  \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) spanning the unit ideal, with each \(D(g)\) (\(g \in t\))
1802	  affine and \(\F|_{D(g)}\) globally generated by finitely many sections. The base case
1803	  \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated} applied on each affine \(D(g)\)
1804	  makes \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) finite over \(\Gamma(X, D(g))\). The locality reduction
1805	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover} then glues these per-member

1809	\begin{lemma}[G3 — flat-locality assembly from per-patch freeness]
1810	  \label{lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly}
1811	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_flat_locality_assembly}
1812	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}
1813	  % LEAN STATUS: G3 — project-built; the geometric glue identifying Γ(F, W∩W_j)
1814	  % localisations across the source cover and the base-restriction is the missing
1815	  % plumbing. Stub-level this iter; built after G1.
1816	  Let \(p : X \to S\) be a finite-type morphism with \(S\) integral and locally
1817	  noetherian, and \(\F\) a finite-type quasi-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Fix a non-empty
1818	  affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\) with \(A\) a noetherian domain, a finite
1819	  affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_j\) of \(p^{-1}(U_0)\), and an element
1820	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that for every \(j\) the localized section module
1821	  \((M_j)_f\) (with \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\)) is free over \(A_f\). Set
1822	  \(V = D(f) \subseteq U_0\). Then for every affine open \(U \le V\) of \(S\) and every
1823	  affine open \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\) of \(X\), the section module \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is flat
1824	  over \(\Gamma(S, U)\).
1825	\end{lemma}
1826	
1827	\begin{proof}
1828	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}
1829	  Free modules are flat (\(\OO\)-flatness of a free module), so each \((M_j)_f\) is flat
1830	  over \(A_f\). Flatness of a module is local on the source: a module is flat over a
1831	  ring iff it is flat after localizing at every maximal ideal, and the family
1832	  \(\{W_j\}\) covers \(p^{-1}(U_0)\), so the per-patch flatness statements assemble to
1833	  flatness of the section module of \(\F\) over \(A_f = \Gamma(S, V)\) on the whole
1834	  preimage \(p^{-1}(V)\). The flat locus is moreover stable under base-restriction along
1835	  the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow V\): for an affine \(U \le V\) the ring
1836	  \(\Gamma(S, U)\) is a localization of \(\Gamma(S, V) = A_f\) (an open immersion of
1837	  affines into a basic open being a localization), and for an affine \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\)
1838	  the module \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is the corresponding localization of the section module
1839	  over the relevant patch \(W_j\) (via \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}
1840	  identifying the finite section modules and their localizations across the cover).
1841	  Flatness is preserved under localization of both the ring and the module, so
1842	  \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is flat over \(\Gamma(S, U)\), as claimed.
1843	\end{proof}

1881	  % ⊔_i Spec ℤ → Spec ℤ with a finite-type torsion sheaf). It is the extra clause
1882	  % promoting `LocallyOfFiniteType` to `FiniteType`, and there is no instance

1884	  % proof prose below invokes it directly (no false "finite type ⟹ quasi-compact" step).
1885	
1886	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''
1887	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1781-L1787).
1888	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1889	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian and integral scheme.

1891	  %  coherent sheaf of \({\mathcal O}_X\)-modules. Then there exists a non-empty
1892	  %  open subscheme \(U\subset S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\)
1893	  %  to \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_U\)."
1894	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
1895	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
1896	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
1897	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
1898	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.
1899	
1900	  The coherence hypothesis on \(\F\) is essential: a non-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module
1901	  need not become flat over any non-empty open of \(S\).
1902	\end{theorem}
1903	
1904	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which
1905	% follows by restricting attention to a non-empty affine open subscheme of \(S\)."
1906	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
1907	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
1908	% the algebraic generic flatness statement (\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic})
1909	% by restricting to a non-empty
1910	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
1911	\begin{proof}
1912	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic, lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections,
1913	        lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly}
1914	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\), where \(A\) is a
1915	  noetherian domain (\(S\) integral and locally noetherian). We assemble the witness
1916	  \(V\) from the algebraic form on a finite affine cover.
1917	
1918	  \emph{Step 1 — finite affine cover above \(U_0\).} Since \(p\) is quasi-compact (by
1919	  the \([\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\,p]\) hypothesis) and \(U_0\) is affine hence
1920	  quasi-compact, the preimage \(X_{U_0} = p^{-1}(U_0)\) is quasi-compact; choose a
1921	  finite affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_{j}\) of \(X_{U_0}\) by affine opens of
1922	  \(X\). Because \(p\) is locally of finite type, each ring \(B_j = \Gamma(X, W_j)\)
1923	  is a finite-type \(A\)-algebra.
1924	
1925	  \emph{Step 2 — read off a finite module on each patch.} On each \(W_j\), quasi-coherence
1926	  of \(\F\) identifies \(\F|_{W_j}\) with the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{M_j}\) of the
1927	  section module \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\), and the finite-section bridge
1928	  \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections} — applied to the affine open \(W_j\) — makes
1929	  \(M_j\) a finite \(B_j\)-module. Through the scalar tower \(A \to B_j \to M_j\),
1930	  \(M_j\) is the data fed to the algebraic form.
1931	
1932	  \emph{Step 3 — apply the algebraic form and take the common basic open.}
1933	  \cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic} applied to \((A, B_j, M_j)\) yields \(f_j \in A\),
1934	  \(f_j \ne 0\), with \((M_j)_{f_j}\) free over \(A_{f_j}\). As the cover is finite, set
1935	  \(f := \prod_j f_j \ne 0\) (a non-zero element of the domain \(A\)) and
1936	  \(V := D(f) \subseteq \Spec A \subseteq S\), a non-empty basic open; over \(V\) every
1937	  \((M_j)_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
1938	
1939	  \emph{Step 4 — freeness implies flatness.} On each patch the localized section module
1940	  is free, hence flat over \(A_f\); the flat-locality assembly
1941	  \cref{lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly} promotes these per-patch freeness statements to
1942	  the fibrewise flatness conclusion, via the criterion that a module flat at every
1943	  maximal ideal is flat together with the locality of flatness on the source cover. This
1944	  produces the required \(V\) with \(\F|_{X_V}\) flat over \(\OO_V\).
1945	\end{proof}

1947	\section{Mathlib status}
1948	\label{sec:flatstrat_mathlib_status}
1949	
1950	Mathlib (master \texttt{b80f227}) provides \texttt{Module.Flat}
1951	(\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic}), the module-theoretic notion of
1952	flatness applicable affine-locally to the section modules of \(\F\), together
1953	with the dévissage and Noether-normalisation infrastructure that the algebraic
1954	generic-flatness argument consumes
1955	(\texttt{IsNoetherianRing.induction\_on\_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime},
1956	\texttt{Module.FinitePresentation.exists\_free\_localizedModule\_powers},
1957	\texttt{exists\_finite\_inj\_algHom\_of\_fg}).  It does \emph{not} yet contain
1958	the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness (a finite module over
1959	\(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) becomes free after inverting a single element), nor the
1960	geometric statement \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
1961	(\cref{thm:generic_flatness}) packaging the algebraic form
1962	(\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}) over a noetherian integral base.  These
1963	are introduced in
1964	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}.

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry

open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry in
theorem gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi {R : CommRingCat} {G F : (Spec R).Modules}
    (π : G ⟶ F) [Epi π] [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
    Function.Surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom := by
  rw [← ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective]
  refine (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ?_
  have hnat := (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓNatTrans (R := R)).naturality π
  simp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.id_map] at hnat
  change (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π) ≫ F.fromTildeΓ
       = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π at hnat
  have h : (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π)
         = (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ :=
    (IsIso.eq_comp_inv F.fromTildeΓ).mpr hnat
  rw [h]
  haveI : Epi (inv F.fromTildeΓ) := inferInstance
  exact epi_comp (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) (inv F.fromTildeΓ)

open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry in
theorem gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated {R : CommRingCat}
    {G F : (Spec R).Modules} (π : G ⟶ F) [Epi π]
    [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ]
    [Module.Finite R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G)] :
    Module.Finite R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
  Module.Finite.of_surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom (gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi π)

#print axioms gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1 base case via the affine tilde–Γ adjunction

The base case of blueprint G1 (`lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`, Stacks 01PB):
on an affine `V ≅ Spec R`, a quasi-coherent sheaf globally generated by finitely
many sections has a finite section module. The crux is **seam 2**
(`gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi`): the affine global-sections functor `moduleSpecΓFunctor`
sends a sheaf epimorphism of quasi-coherent modules to a *surjective* `R`-module
map. This is the structural content of "H¹(affine, qcoh) = 0" delivered for free by
Mathlib's affine `tilde.adjunction` counit (`fromTildeΓNatTrans`) — no cohomology
build. Recipe: `analogies/gf-gamma-exact.md`. -/

/-- **Seam 2 — `Γ` on affine quasi-coherent modules sends epis to surjections.**
For `G, F : (Spec R).Modules` with both tilde–Γ counits `IsIso` (the quasi-coherent
hypothesis, supplied by `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict` /
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`) and `π : G ⟶ F` an epimorphism of sheaves of
modules, the affine global-sections map `(moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom` is a
surjective `R`-linear map.

Mechanism (no cohomology): the counit `fromTildeΓNatTrans` is natural in `π`, so
`~(Γπ) = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ` — a composite of an iso, the epi `π`,
and an iso, hence an epi. `tilde.functor R` is faithful, so it reflects epimorphisms;
therefore `Γπ` is epi in `ModuleCat R`, i.e. surjective. Project-local: Mathlib's
`tilde.adjunction` supplies every ingredient but not this packaged corollary. -/
theorem gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {G F : (Spec R).Modules}
    (π : G ⟶ F) [Epi π] [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
    Function.Surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom := by
  rw [← ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective]
  refine (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ?_
  have hnat := (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓNatTrans (R := R)).naturality π
  simp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.id_map] at hnat
  change (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π) ≫ F.fromTildeΓ
       = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π at hnat
  have h : (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π)
         = (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ :=
    (IsIso.eq_comp_inv F.fromTildeΓ).mpr hnat
  rw [h]
  haveI : Epi (inv F.fromTildeΓ) := inferInstance
  exact epi_comp (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) (inv F.fromTildeΓ)

/-- **Seam 3 — a finitely globally-generated qcoh sheaf has finite affine sections.**
If `π : G ⟶ F` is an epimorphism of quasi-coherent `(Spec R)`-modules (both counits
`IsIso`) and the source section module `Γ(G, ⊤) = moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G` is finite
over `R`, then the target section module `Γ(F, ⊤)` is finite over `R`.

Immediate from seam 2 (`gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi`): the surjective `R`-linear map
`Γ(π)` out of a finite module forces its image to be finite
(`Module.Finite.of_surjective`). The caller instantiates `G` as a finite free
`O_V^{⊕I}` (`I` finite), whose `Γ` is `R^I`, finite; this is the base case of G1.
Project-local. -/
theorem gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    {G F : (Spec R).Modules} (π : G ⟶ F) [Epi π]
    [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ]
    [Module.Finite R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G)] :
    Module.Finite R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
  Module.Finite.of_surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom (gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi π)

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added: 3** (all axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi` (seam 2, the crux) — line ~2272
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated` (seam 3) — line ~2300
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi` (seam 3, self-contained base case) — line ~2325
- **Declarations blocked: 2** — `gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated` (seam 1) and
  `gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections` (G1 assembly). Both need Mathlib-absent geometric infra (below).
- **sorry count in file: 1 → 1** (the pre-existing `genericFlatness` @ ~2360, unchanged — downstream of G1).

## gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi (seam 2, crux) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** Followed `analogies/gf-gamma-exact.md` recipe to the letter, with two corrections the
  recipe's `simp`/`rw` glue needed:
  1. `fromTildeΓNatTrans.app M` is only *defeq* (not syntactically) to `M.fromTildeΓ`; `‹IsIso M.fromTildeΓ›`
     does NOT discharge `IsIso (…app M)` directly. Fix: `change … at hnat` to rewrite the naturality square
     into `…fromTildeΓ` form (defeq `change` works; `‹›`/`simp` do not).
  2. `Category.assoc`/`IsIso.hom_inv_id` `rw`/`simp only` silently fail to fire on the `(Spec R).Modules`
     composition in the `inv`-cancellation step. Fix: use the iso-cancellation lemma directly as a TERM:
     `(IsIso.eq_comp_inv F.fromTildeΓ).mpr hnat` (note `eq_comp_inv` takes the iso `α` EXPLICIT).
  3. Final epi: `infer_instance` for `Epi (… ≫ … ≫ …)` TIMES OUT (20k heartbeats). Use explicit
     `epi_comp (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) (inv F.fromTildeΓ)` with `haveI : Epi (inv F.fromTildeΓ) := inferInstance`.
- **Mathlib anchors (all verified present):** `moduleSpecΓFunctor`, `tilde.functor R` (Fully Faithful →
  ReflectsEpimorphisms → `Functor.epi_of_epi_map`), `Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓNatTrans` (`.naturality`),
  `ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective`, `IsIso.eq_comp_inv`, `epi_comp`.

## gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated (seam 3) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** 1-liner: `Module.Finite.of_surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom (gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi π)`.
  Takes `[Module.Finite R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G)]` as hypothesis (source-section finiteness).

## gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi (seam 3 base case, self-contained) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** Discharges seam-3's source hypotheses for a *finite free* source `G = tilde N`, `N` finite:
  - `IsIso (tilde N).fromTildeΓ` = `IsIso (tilde.adjunction.counit.app (tilde.obj N))`, an instance because
    `tilde.functor R` is a fully-faithful LEFT adjoint (counit iso on its image). `tilde.adjunction.counit
    = fromTildeΓNatTrans` by `rfl`.
  - `Module.Finite R (Γ(tilde N))` from the adjunction UNIT iso `N ≅ Γ(tilde N)` (L fully faithful ⟹ unit
    iso): `Module.Finite.of_surjective (unit.app N).hom (epi⟹surjective)`. NB needed
    `haveI : Module.Finite R ((𝟭 _).obj N) := inferInstanceAs …` — `(𝟭).obj N` is only defeq `N`.
- **Why it matters:** complete Stacks-01PB affine base case ("globally generated by finitely many sections ⟹
  finite sections") with the generation datum presented as a finite-free tilde-epi. No transport needed at
  the `Spec R` level — it is fully self-contained and ready to consume once a free epi is produced.

## gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated (seam 1) — NOT ADDED (genuine Mathlib-absent gap)
- **What it needs:** from `F.IsFiniteType` (`SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType` = `∃ σ : F.LocalGeneratorsData,
  σ.IsFiniteType`) on `X.Modules`, refine the abstract cover `σ.X : σ.I → X.Opens` (arbitrary opens,
  `σ.coversTop`, each carrying a *finite* `GeneratingSections` of `F.over (σ.X i)`) to a FINITE BASIC-OPEN
  cover `{D(g) : g ∈ t ⊆ Γ(X,W)}` of a given affine `W`, preserving finite generation under restriction.
- **Missing primitives (each a Mathlib gap):** (1) restriction/refinement of a `GeneratingSections` /
  `LocalGeneratorsData` along an open immersion `D(g) ↪ σ.X i` (generation survives, finiteness preserved);
  (2) topological refinement of an arbitrary open cover of an affine `W ≅ Spec Γ(X,W)` by standard basic
  opens (`PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`) + quasi-compactness (`W` affine ⟹ compact) to a FINITE
  subfamily; (3) re-expressing "finite generation on D(g)" as a free epi `O_{D(g)}^{⊕I} ↠ F|_{D(g)}`.
- **Confirmed no shortcut:** `lean_leansearch`/`lean_loogle` over `SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType` /
  "qcoh finite type affine ⟹ finite module" return only the abstract predicate constructors — Mathlib has NO
  affine-local finiteness bridge at this pin. No LLM key in env (per PROGRESS.md) → informal agent unavailable.

## gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections (G1 assembly) — NOT ADDED (blocked on seam 1 + X.Modules↔Spec transport)
- **Path is otherwise complete:** locality half `gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover` (iter-045,
  DONE) + per-D(g) base case. Two missing links:
  1. **seam 1** (above) to produce the finite basic-open cover + per-member free epi.
  2. **Transport `X.Modules|_{D(g)} ↝ (Spec Γ(X,D(g))).Modules`** so the Spec-level base case
     `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi` yields `Module.Finite Γ(X,D(g)) Γ(F,D(g))` (the locality half's
     per-element hypothesis). This is the `overRestrictEquiv`/opaque-immersion plumbing that QUOT gap1/gap2
     went through (memories `quot-gap1-closed-opaque-immersion`, `sheafofmodules-slice-presentation-transport-tricks`)
     — heavy (form-coercion `↥V`/`↥↑V`, `IsContinuous` non-synth, whnf runaway risk). My seam-2/3 are stated
     over `(Spec R).Modules` precisely because seam 2's proof IS the affine `tilde.adjunction`; the transport
     is the price of moving to `X.Modules`.
- **Next step (recommended decomposition for next iter):**
  (a) Build seam 1 as 3 sub-lemmas matching the 3 missing primitives above (the topological refinement (2)
      is the most self-contained — start there).
  (b) Build an `X.Modules` affine-local restatement of `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi` via the QUOT
      `overRestrictEquiv` transport (reuse the gap1 opaque-immersion recipe; keep the affine immersion OPAQUE
      to avoid whnf runaway).
  (c) Assemble: seam 1 → (b) per D(g) → locality half ⟹ `gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`.

## Needs blueprint entry
All 3 added decls already have blueprint blocks (planner pre-blueprinted this lane), so the Lean↔blueprint
correspondence is NOT broken — but ONE decl is NEW (not in the planned 4) and needs a block:
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi`** — NEW helper, no blueprint block yet.
  It is seam 3 (`lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated`) specialised to a finite-free `tilde N`
  source, discharging the two source-side hypotheses (`IsIso (tilde N).fromTildeΓ` via fully-faithful-left-
  adjoint counit; `Module.Finite R Γ(tilde N)` via the unit iso). Proof relies on: `tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor`,
  `tilde.adjunction` (counit `= fromTildeΓNatTrans` by rfl; unit iso for fully-faithful L), `Module.Finite.of_surjective`,
  `ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective`. Suggest a `\lean{}` pin under `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated`
  (it is the concrete realisation of "ℴ_V^I ↠ F" the blueprint statement mentions).
- The 3 planned decls (`gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi`, `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated`,
  `gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated`) keep their existing `\lean{}` pins. seam-1 + G1-assembly
  blocks remain unformalized (no `\leanok` — they are not in the file).

## Why I stopped
- **Partial progress:** 3 axiom-clean declarations added (`gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi` ~L2272,
  `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated` ~L2300, `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi` ~L2325),
  including the flagged crux seam 2. Blocked on the next two (seam 1 + G1 assembly) by a genuine
  Mathlib-absent geometric gap: (i) refining `SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType`'s abstract open cover to a finite
  basic-open cover of an affine with finite generation preserved (3 missing primitives, named above), and
  (ii) the `X.Modules|_{affine} ↔ (Spec Γ).Modules` transport (the QUOT opaque-immersion plumbing) needed to
  feed the Spec-level base case into the `X.Modules` locality half. Both were searched (no Mathlib bridge);
  no LLM key available. This is exactly the seam-1 STOP-and-flag the PROGRESS.md WATCH note anticipated.
- **No approach left un-attempted:** seam 2 recipe fully executed (3 glue fixes); seam 3 + free-epi base case
  built beyond the planned scope; seam 1 confirmed to need new infra by direct API search, not assumed.
